title: Rocket Lab's Biggest Launch Deal Yet: Confidential Customer Books Multiple Neutron and Electron Launches
text: Rocket Lab announced its largest launch contract in company history with an undisclosed customer. The agreement encompasses eight dedicated missions: five Neutron launches and three Electron launches, scheduled between 2026 and 2029. Launches will occur from facilities in New Zealand and Virginia. This contract brings Rocket Lab's total launch manifest to over 70 missions and increases the company's backlog to more than $2.2 billion. According to CEO Sir Peter Beck, the space industry needs more launch capacity and it needs it from launch providers who know how to deliver. The company has now completed 87 Electron launches and achieved remarkable momentum, selling more launches in the first quarter of 2026 than during all of 2025. Rocket Lab positions itself as the world's second most active launch provider, utilizing a three-vehicle strategy: Electron for small satellites, HASTE for hypersonic testing, and Neutron for medium-lift commercial and national security missions.
